name: Cape Sant Antoni and the Windmills Trail
description: Hike the moderate 4.6-mile (7.4 km) Miradores de Jávea circular route for stunning coastal views, pine forests, and historical windmills.
region_intro: Hiking around Jávea/Xàbia offers diverse terrain along the Costa Blanca, characterized by its rugged coastline, limestone cliffs, and the prominent Montgó Massif. The region features a mix of Mediterranean forests, coves, and elevated viewpoints. These physical features provide varied landscapes for exploration. Best hiking trails around Jávea/Xàbia The most popular hiking route is La Granadella (Circular PR-CV 354), an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores coastal areas and Mediterranean forest. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Montgó — circular from Jávea, a difficult 9.4 miles (15.2 km) path. This trail ascends the Montgó mountain, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through coastal scenery and historical windmills, often completed in about 1 hour 9 minutes. Hiking around Jávea/Xàbia is defined by coastal paths, limestone formations, and elevated viewpoints. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Jávea/Xàbia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 2000 reviews. More than 11000 hikers have used komoot to explore Jávea/Xàbia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Jávea/Xàbia offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 240 routes recorded on komoot. These range from easy coastal strolls to challenging mountain ascents, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: The ideal time for hiking in Jávea/Xàbia is during the &lt;strong&gt;spring&lt;/strong&gt; (March to May) and &lt;strong&gt;autumn&lt;/strong&gt; (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is pleasantly mild, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Yes, Jávea/Xàbia has numerous easy trails perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222917773/cape-san-antonio-windmills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.7-mile (4.4 km) route that takes just over an hour, offering gentle coastal scenery and historical windmills.

Q: Where can I find challenging hikes around Jávea/Xàbia?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the region's prominent Montgó Massif offers demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870232585/el-montgo-circular-from-javea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Montgó — circular from Jávea&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.4-mile (15.2 km) path that ascends the mountain, providing panoramic views of the surrounding landscape and coastline.

Q: What are some good family-friendly hiking trails in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Many trails in Jávea/Xàbia are suitable for families. Look for routes with shorter distances and less elevation gain. Coastal paths and trails around coves often provide engaging scenery without being too strenuous. Always check the route details for specific difficulty and length to ensure it suits your family's needs.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes available in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Yes, Jávea/Xàbia features a good selection of circular routes, allowing you to start and end your hike at the same point. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e867445173/la-granadella-circular-pr-cv-354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Granadella (Circular PR-CV 354)&lt;/a&gt;, which explores coastal areas and Mediterranean forest.

Q: Which hiking trails in Jávea/Xàbia offer the best viewpoints?
A: Jávea/Xàbia is renowned for its stunning viewpoints. Trails ascending the Montgó Massif, such as the circular route from Jávea, offer expansive vistas. Additionally, coastal paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1223030909/cape-sant-antoni-and-the-windmills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape Sant Antoni and the Windmills Trail&lt;/a&gt; provide breathtaking views over the Mediterranean Sea and the bay.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely! Jávea/Xàbia's trails often lead to or pass by significant landmarks. You can explore natural wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt;, or historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/203776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Jávea&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/416894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Gerro&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer opportunities to discover these points of interest.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points, especially near beaches or natural parks, have designated parking areas, though these can fill up quickly during peak season. It's advisable to check specific route information or arrive early, particularly for well-known trails.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Jávea/Xàbia, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 2000 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse coastal scenery, the dramatic limestone formations, and the rewarding elevated viewpoints. The variety of trails, from easy strolls to challenging ascents, is also a common highlight.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Jávea/Xàbia?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Jávea/Xàbia, especially in natural areas. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in protected zones or areas with wildlife. Some specific natural parks or reserves might have restrictions, so checking local signage is recommended.

Q: Do you have any tips for hiking in Jávea/Xàbia during the summer?
A: Hiking in Jávea/Xàbia during summer requires careful planning due to high temperatures. We recommend starting your hikes very early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat. Always carry plenty of water, wear sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and choose shaded routes or coastal paths where a sea breeze might offer some relief.
tips: An all-round highlight. Not too hard

The lighthouse that overlooks the bay from Cap de Sant Antoni was the successor to other defensive structures that took advantage of the privileged location of the plain. Hermits built the chapel of San Antonio in the 14th century to isolate themselves from secular life. It was built in his honor and eventually gave the relief its name. A watchtower to protect against pirate raids was also built on this ground, which later gave way to the current lighthouse. This viewpoint is without a doubt one of the most privileged locations on the Valencian coast.
From this viewpoint you will have the best views of Jávea and Denia! A unique place with amazing views, ideal throughout the day but especially recommended at sunrise or sunset!

The remaining ruins of the former wheat mills still bear witness to the time when agriculture was of greater importance in the region. Some of them are now privately owned.
It is not known exactly when the mills were built. The last reference to the company probably dates back to 1911.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on the Miradores de Jávea circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is considered of moderate difficulty, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. The terrain is varied, featuring a mix of accessible paths, rocky ground, and some ramps. You'll traverse paths through dense pine woods, offering refreshing shade, alongside sections with open coastal views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along this route?
faqs.answer: The route is renowned for its spectacular panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea, dramatic cliffs, and picturesque coves. Key highlights include the View of the Bay of Jávea / Xàbia, the historic Cap de Sant Antoni Lighthouse, and a broad View of Jávea, Cape Sant Antoni, and Montgó. You'll also pass the ancient Windmills of Jávea, which offer their own panoramic vistas, and the unique Stairway to Jávea.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Miradores de Jávea circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for a single 'Miradores de Jávea circular route' trailhead aren't universally defined, many hikers start near prominent miradores or access points along the coast. It's advisable to check local signage for designated parking spots, especially near popular viewpoints like Cap de la Nau or Cap de Sant Antoni, or in residential areas with public parking.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Miradores de Jávea circular route. The trail is located within the Parc Natural del Montgó, which is freely accessible to the public. However, always respect natural park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Is the Miradores de Jávea circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through the Parc Natural del Montgó, dogs must be kept on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Be mindful of rocky sections and potential heat, especially during warmer months, and ensure your dog has access to water.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural vegetation is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable if visiting then. Winter offers pleasant temperatures but can sometimes bring rain.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. Significant portions of the route overlap with El Camí IT5TR1 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana (79% coverage) and Xàbia - Montgo (38% coverage). Smaller sections also connect with Camino del Alba (17%), Xàbia - Cala Tango (16%), and Camí Monastir (7%).

faqs.question: Are there facilities or amenities available along the route?
faqs.answer: The route itself is primarily natural, so dedicated facilities like restrooms or water fountains are scarce directly on the trail. However, as it passes through and near Jávea, you'll find cafes, restaurants, and shops in the town and surrounding areas. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for your hike.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, wear comfortable hiking shoes with good grip, as some sections can be rocky. Dress in layers suitable for the weather, and bring sun protection (hat, sunglasses, sunscreen) as much of the route is exposed. Always carry enough water, especially during warmer months, and a fully charged phone for navigation or emergencies.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While the overall route is rated moderate, individual miradores are often easily accessible. For families, consider breaking the route into shorter, easier sections or driving to specific viewpoints and enjoying shorter walks. The full 7.43 km route with its varied terrain and elevation might be challenging for very young children or those not accustomed to hiking.

faqs.question: Can I see Ibiza from any of the viewpoints on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, on exceptionally clear days, it is possible to catch a glimpse of the island of Ibiza from certain elevated viewpoints along the Miradores de Jávea route, particularly from spots like Mirador del Cap de la Nau. These sightings are not guaranteed but add to the magic of the panoramic views.
